Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to health, well-being, or related targets.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (4.3). It explicitly cultivates critical thinking (4.4) and understanding of diverse cultural perspectives through literature (4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: The course includes literary works like 'Daisy Miller' and 'Eveline,' which often involve analyzing female characters and societal roles, fostering critical perspectives on gender equality (Target 5.1).

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to energy access or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Developing 'independent and critical judgment' and strong writing skills are transferable to professional work. 'Respect Copyright' aligns with ethical practices in creative industries (Target 8.8).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course fosters understanding of 'different cultural perspectives' (Core Competence A) and includes 'My Son the Fanatic,' which explores themes of inequality based on origin and religion (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Studying Western literature and its historical context (Core Competence A) contributes to understanding and safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4).
